evals.prompt: I want to research Claude Code automation — can you build me a notebook on it in NotebookLM, pull in some web sources, and give me a quick summary of what's there?
evals.expected_output: Creates a notebook titled something like 'Claude Code Automation', runs web research, imports sources, and returns a summary with notebook ID and source count. Offers next steps like generating a podcast or querying.

assertions.name: calls-notebook-create
assertions.description: Uses mcp__notebooklm__notebook_create or notebook_list to set up or locate a notebook

assertions.name: runs-research-or-adds-sources
assertions.description: Calls research_start or source_add to populate the notebook with sources

assertions.name: returns-notebook-id
assertions.description: The response includes a notebook ID

assertions.name: provides-summary
assertions.description: Gives a meaningful summary of what was found/added, not just a blank confirmation

assertions.name: offers-next-steps
assertions.description: Suggests what Wayne can do next (query, podcast, etc.)

evals.expected_output: Lists all existing notebooks with their titles, IDs, and source counts in a readable format.

assertions.name: formatted-output
assertions.description: Returns notebooks in a readable table or list format, not raw JSON

assertions.name: shows-source-count
assertions.description: Shows how many sources each notebook has

evals.prompt: make a podcast from my most recently updated NotebookLM notebook
evals.expected_output: Finds the most recent notebook, kicks off audio generation (deep_dive format), polls until complete, and returns a listen URL.

assertions.name: finds-notebook
assertions.description: Calls notebook_list to identify the most recently updated notebook

assertions.name: polls-for-completion
assertions.description: Calls studio_status to check generation progress

assertions.name: returns-url-or-status
assertions.description: Returns a listen URL or clearly states the generation status
